definition: Passion or affective coloring that heats and inclines the mind toward its object.
notes: The project keeps passion as the default because it preserves the heated, affectively colored force of rāga without reducing it to merely sexual lust. Attachment remains a controlled alternate when the sticking quality needs emphasis, and coloring remains available only when the text or explanation explicitly brings out that image. In this repository, rāga is not the same as taṇhā: taṇhā is ignorant wanting, whereas rāga is the passionate coloring or heat that accompanies attraction. It is also not the same as chanda, which can be wholesome or neutral, nor as nandī, which names relishing or delighting, nor as upādāna, which names appropriation or taking personally. The formula nandirāgasahagatā should preserve both relishing and passion instead of collapsing them into one English word. The OSF glossary supports keeping rāga broader than sexual desire, and MN 64 supports the liberation compounds and latent-tendency connections built from the term.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and practical contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing sticking or adhesive pull rather than heat
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the line wants the sticking dimension.

context_rules.context: In close explanatory prose drawing out the image of the mind being tinged or dyed
context_rules.notes: Use selectively; it is not the house default.

example_phrases.notes: Compact doctrinal compound showing the term in liberation language.
Useful bridge to the residue family without collapsing the categories.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping passion as the house default while blocking reduction of rāga to merely sexual lust.
Supports the liberation compound rāgakkhaya and the residue-family link rāgānusaya.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'passion' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'attachment' or 'coloring' only when the local line clearly needs that narrower shade. Do not collapse rāga into generic wanting, craving, or pleasure-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents rāga from collapsing into taṇhā-language, chanda-language, or generic lust language.
